page: Amazon Web Services c7i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz, with 32 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor architecture includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It has no local storage or GPU accelerators, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c7i.16xlarge demonstrates strong performance in compute-intensive and memory-cached operations. It achieves a PassMark CPU score of 70832.71 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17805.0, placing both in the top 25% tier. Its memory subsystem shows a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 4632.0 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier at 174.23 GB/sec and 75.45 GB/sec respectively. The hardware-accelerated instruction sets contribute to strong results in cryptography, database operations, and static web serving, where it processes 72.7 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers 70.64 GB/sec of web serving throughput.

The instance exhibits a clear tradeoff between raw compute capabilities and storage or memory capacity. With no local NVMe storage and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it is not configured for massive local data persistence or memory-bound datasets. However, its high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon cores make it efficient for workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference. For example, it processes 247.69 tokens per second for medium-sized 7B LLM prompt processing, though text generation for large 70B models drops to an average-tier 3.69 tokens per second.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), driving strong benchmark results in the top 25% tier, including a PassMark CPU score of 70832.71 and 72.7 million Redis SET operations per second. While uncached memory write speeds of 75.45 GB/sec remain in the average tier, the instance excels in compute-heavy tasks. It is suited for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, cryptographic processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for CPU-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 64-vCPU compute-optimized platform with DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput processing and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong performance in software compilation, cryptography, and Redis operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
